mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling and CPIAUCSL is stable or decelerating, discount-rate pressure eases faster than inflation expectations rise, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is flat or lower, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals slowing household income and weaker risk appetite, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month as labor-market deterioration becomes visible to investors.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses all directional edge after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained advance in RSAFS and HOUST indicates resilient consumption and housing demand, supporting revenue expectations and equity breadth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous month-over-month gains in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating INDPRO after a prior soft patch signals reacceleration in industrial supply capacity and earnings leverage for cyclicals, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if rebound episodes in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the effect reverses in more than half of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series alongside falling UNRATE indicates labor-income expansion and stronger aggregate demand, which should support near-term equity prices through improved sales expectations and lower recession probability.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE falls over the latest month
failure_condition: Reject if monthly payroll gains coupled with lower unemployment fail to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined signal is no better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level suppresses equity multiples by raising the discount rate on future cash flows, so when policy is above a restrictive threshold the expected 20-day S&P 500 return is negative.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day returns are not negative on average during restrictive-rate episodes, or if forward returns remain positive in more than 40% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is already weak and housing starts remain depressed, the market should price a higher recession probability and lower earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below 1% annualized and HOUST is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if weak-GDP/weak-housing episodes do not precede negative 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction signal is neutral after walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-unemployment-spike recovery, falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market healing and cyclical optimism, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if recovery episodes defined by falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS do not outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if positive returns are not observed in at least 55% of triggered cases.
